title: session:resume writes state file to wrong path (missing state/ subdir)
summary: The session:resume CLI path can write the resumed session state file to <state-dir>/<sessionId>.md when passed a root-style --state-dir such as .a5c or ~/.a5c, while hooks normalize and read <root>/state/<sessionId>.md. The fix should make the writer and hook reader share canonical session state directory semantics.
acceptanceFocus: session:resume with explicit --state-dir equal to the configured global root writes <root>/state/<sessionId>.md, not <root>/<sessionId>.md.
session:resume with documented relative --state-dir .a5c writes the same canonical path the stop hook reads when .a5c is the configured/global state root.
The JSON stateFile and human output show the actual canonical file path written.
The state file contains active: true and the requested run_id so the Stop hook can resume blocking/iteration decisions.
Intentionally custom leaf session state directories continue to work, unless maintainer guidance explicitly changes that contract.
Hook/adapters and session CLI commands agree on root-style state-dir normalization.
qualityGates: Regression tests fail before implementation and pass after implementation.
Targeted session resume and lifecycle tests pass.
SDK test suite passes or any pre-existing unrelated failures are documented with evidence.
Reviewer approves path normalization semantics and compatibility with existing custom state directory behavior.
nonGoals: Do not change hooks-mux package behavior; the issue is in SDK session state-dir resolution.
Do not change run directory resolution or journal replay semantics.
Do not implement broad recovery for missing session files; issue #166 tracks that related but separate problem.
Do not add shell-oriented subtasks to this repository process.

knownContext.issueObservation: Current session:resume uses args.stateDir directly before getSessionFilePath, while hooks/adapters use normalizeSessionStateDir(args.stateDir ?? BABYSITTER_STATE_DIR).
knownContext.compatibilityBoundary: Root-style stateDir values should normalize to <root>/state; already-canonical or intentionally custom leaf directories should remain direct session state directories.
